Title: v0.2.4 — Auto-update
Summary: Bleepforge now auto-updates itself via electron-updater + GitHub Releases. Two-stage toast UX: a brief 'downloading' info toast when a new version is detected, then a sticky cyan 'ready to install — click to restart' toast when the download completes. Toast component gained `persistent` (no auto-dismiss) + `onClick` (callback in place of route navigation) options to support the new flow.
Body:
## Headlines

### Auto-update via electron-updater + GitHub Releases

v0.2.4+ installs check for updates automatically on every launch (5 seconds after the main window opens, so the check doesn't race with server boot or splash). When a new version is found, `electron-updater` downloads it in the background; when the download finishes, you get a click-to-restart toast.

**Update source**: GitHub Releases of `ymoraviadev-droid/bleepforge` via the new `publish` config in `electron-builder.json`. Every `pnpm dist` now emits a `latest.yml` (Windows) or `latest-linux.yml` (Linux) alongside the installer — those metadata files are what `electron-updater` reads to decide whether a download is needed.

**Dev mode skipped**: there's no `app-update.yml` inside an unpackaged build, so the updater short-circuits in dev (`pnpm dev:desktop`) and unpackaged runs. No noisy errors, just silent no-op.

**Windows binaries still unsigned**: per the no-paid-services constraint, we don't ship signed binaries. Auto-update still works on Windows — but every update install triggers a fresh SmartScreen warning the user has to click through. Accepted UX cost. Linux AppImages don't have a comparable warning, so the experience there is fully seamless.

### Two-stage toast UX

```
update-available  → 6s info toast: "Update v0.2.5 available — downloading…"
                    (auto-dismisses; ambient cue while the download runs)

update-downloaded → sticky cyan toast: "v0.2.5 ready to install — click to restart"
                    (no auto-dismiss; survives until clicked or × dismissed)
```

Clicking the ready-to-install toast triggers `autoUpdater.quitAndInstall()` — the app closes, the installer runs (NSIS on Windows, AppImage replacement on Linux), the new version launches.

**Silent events**: `checking`, `not-available`, `download-progress`, and `error` deliberately stay silent on the UI. Each one would just be noise. The full trace is in `<userData>/boot.log` for debugging.

**Per-window toasts**: both the main window AND open popouts (Diagnostics / Help / Preferences) receive the update events and render their own toast. You'll see the prompt wherever you're focused.

### Toast component extensions

Two new options on `pushToast` to support the click-to-restart flow:

- **`persistent: true`** — skips the auto-dismiss timer entirely. The toast survives until the user clicks the × or activates it via `to` / `onClick`. The progress bar is hidden too (no countdown to show). Reserved for genuinely action-required toasts; using it everywhere would defeat the timer's job.
- **`onClick: () => void`** — imperative callback in place of `to` (route navigation). The toast renders as a `<button>` instead of a `<Link>`. Mutually exclusive with `to`. Used by the update-ready toast to invoke `installUpdate()` rather than navigate to a page.

## Rollout reality

Auto-update can't be retrofitted onto installed binaries that don't already ship the updater. So:

- **v0.2.3 → v0.2.4**: requires a manual reinstall (one last time).
- **v0.2.4 → v0.2.5+**: handled automatically by the new updater.

Maor + anyone else on a pre-v0.2.4 install will need a fresh download to get on the auto-update track.

## Under the hood

- **Preload bridge** (`electron/src/preload.ts`) gained `onUpdaterStatus(cb)` returning an unsubscribe function, and `installUpdate()` invoking the new `updater:install` IPC. Type-safe payload shared between main and renderer.
- **Main process** (`electron/src/main.ts`) broadcasts `updater:status` IPC to every open window alongside the existing `bootLog` calls. Both targets get every event; popouts and main render the same toast independently from their own toast bus.
- **Renderer hook** (`client/src/lib/updater/useUpdaterToasts.ts`) mounts at App root next to the three existing toast bridges (sync / shader / outgoing-save). No-op in browser mode.

## Known issues / deferred

- **No manual 'Check for updates' button**. The boot check covers 95% of the case for an app you open daily; on-demand check is a candidate for a future cycle if it turns out to matter.
- **No opt-out**. Auto-update is always on for v0.2.4+. Adding a Preferences toggle would be premature for a single-user tool; users can decline the install toast or close it.
- **macOS packaging + auto-update + code signing** still deferred. Linux AppImage + Windows NSIS are the only targets shipping today.
- **Genericize for any Godot project** — long-term anchor (v0.2.5+). The seven game-domain schemas + per-domain edit forms + per-domain `.tres` mappers are still hardcoded to Flock of Bleeps.

> tip: see [v0.2.3](/help/release-notes/v0-2-3) for the Windows NSIS installer + welcome-flow work this release builds on.
